full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic hinge-arm (a.k.a. 'petal root') for a deployable hex-panel antenna per the NASA 'Let It Go (After Latching)' open-CAD challenge. The full antenna consists of 6 identical hexagonal panels arranged around a central hub; each panel hinges from stowed (folded flat against hub) to deployed (co-planar hexagonal aperture). This prompt is for the hinge-arm that connects one panel to the central hub. Geometric constraints: stowed envelope height 250 mm, stowed diameter 600 mm; deployed aperture 1500 mm flat-to-flat (hex); hinge arm spans 300 mm hub-to-panel with a 2-pin knuckle joint (two M5 pins); panel interface is a 60 mm x 60 mm flange with 4x M4 tapped holes. Arm bounding 300 mm x 50 mm x 25 mm. Material: 6061-T6 aluminum alloy, hard-anodized (E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, ultimate = 310 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3). Load cases: (R1) Launch lock-down quasi-static 25 g triaxial on the panel mass (panel mass 1.2 kg) = 294 N triaxial at panel flange; max von Mises in hinge arm <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa; deflection at panel flange <= 2 mm. (R2) Deploy-latch shock: 50 g half-sine 5 ms at the moment of deploy-latch, lumped as 588 N axial impulse at panel flange; max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 207 MPa. (R3) On-orbit solar-pressure + thermal bending of the 1.5 m deployed aperture contributing 2 N*m edge moment at panel flange; max von Mises <= 184 MPa; arm tip rotation <= 0.5 mrad (pointing budget). (R4) Thermal cycle -150 C to +120 C (LEO sun-shadow), CTE-induced stress on the pinned joint kept elastic (static residual stress <= 184 MPa). (R5) Mass of arm <= 120 g. (R6) First modal frequency hard-mounted at hub side, panel mass lumped at flange >= 40 Hz (to survive launch random-vibration spectrum). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3 + thermal x1) + modal. Vibration secondary via R6. Fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid hinge arm, hinge-pin knuckle drawing, and a report listing arm mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, panel flange deflection in mm, arm tip rotation in mrad, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic hinge-arm ('petal root') connecting one hexagonal panel to the central hub of a 6-panel deployable antenna per the NASA 'Let It Go (After Latching)' open-CAD challenge. Stowed 250 mm tall x 600 mm diameter; deployed 1500 mm flat-to-flat aperture; panel mass 1.2 kg.

Envelope: arm 300 mm x 50 mm x 25 mm with a 2-pin knuckle joint (M5 pins) on the hub side and a 60 x 60 mm 4xM4 panel flange on the other.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 for quasi-static launch, ultimate FoS 1.5 for deploy-latch shock; launch-survivable first-mode frequency target set to decouple from random-vib.

Load cases.
- Launch lockdown quasi-static 25 g triaxial on 1.2 kg panel = 294 N triaxial at the panel flange.
- Deploy-latch shock: 50 g half-sine 5 ms = 588 N axial impulse at the panel flange.
- On-orbit solar-pressure + thermal edge moment 2 N-m at panel flange; arm-tip rotation target <= 0.5 mrad (pointing budget).
- Thermal cycle -150 C to +120 C (LEO sun-shadow), CTE-induced residual on the pinned joint.
- Hub-fixed, panel-lumped first modal frequency >= 40 Hz.

prompt.title: NASA Let It Go hinge arm
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 monolithic hinge arm for 6-panel deployable hex antenna, launch 25 g + deploy 50 g shock + thermal cycle.

boundary_conditions.mounting: hub side of arm fully fixed

eval_coverage: deployable-hinge integration with prior latch CAD
